generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance SINUMERIK840DController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller executing control algorithms (position, speed, or acceleration inputs) and generating motor current (I_M) as primary actuation input for the MABI MAX robot's first axis."
]

instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Converts electrical energy (current) into torque (τ_M) for mechanical motion transmission through gearwheel and cycloidal gearbox."
]

instance GearWheel : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical component transmitting torque from motor to cycloidal gearbox, contributing to mechanical motion."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Transmits torque from gearwheel to achieve precise mechanical motion of the first axis with reduced backlash."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the motor providing real-time angular position data for torque/velocity calculations."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind the gearbox providing real-time angular position data for backlash compensation and elasticity analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures motor current (I_M) used to derive torque (τ_M) via motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram describing structural composition (Motor, Gearbox, CycloidalGearbox) and energy flows (Current, Rotational)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLTool
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram detailing behavioral relationships and energy flows between components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLTool
]

instance ManufacturerDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamics models provided by manufacturer to compute torque and angular velocity from encoder data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Friction model implemented in Simulink to compute Coulomb-Viscous friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Friction model implemented in Simulink to compute Stribeck friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Friction model implemented in Simulink to compute Dahl friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Friction model implemented in Simulink to compute LuGre friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance FrictionlessMotorSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink simulation model of motor/gearbox without friction as baseline for comparison."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance SimulationMotor : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Processing component combining measured data and friction models to generate simulated motor torque (τ̂_M) and angular velocity (ω̂_M)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance SimulationGearWheel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Processing component combining measured data and friction models to generate simulated gearwheel torque (τ̂_G) and angular velocity (ω̂_G)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance MeasuredCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured motor current (I_M) from MotorCurrentSensor."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance MeasuredMotorTorqueVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived motor torque (τ_M) and angular velocity (ω_M) from encoder data using manufacturer dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance MeasuredGearWheelTorqueVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived gearwheel torque (τ_G) and angular velocity (ω_G) from secondary encoder data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance SimulatedMotorTorqueVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated motor torque (τ̂_M) and angular velocity (ω̂_M) with friction effects subtracted."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance SimulatedGearWheelTorqueVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated gearwheel torque (τ̂_G) and angular velocity (ω̂_G) with friction effects subtracted."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance SimulatedOutputTorqueVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated output torque (τ̂_out) and angular velocity (ω̂_out) representing friction-compensated system behavior."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance DataTraceMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Metadata including source (sensor/channel), timestamp, and processing component origin for all data traces."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AssetAdministrationShell
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling tool for structural/behavioral modeling via Block Definition Diagrams and Internal Block Diagrams."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, ModelValidationService, RootCauseAnalysisService
]

instance SimulinkTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink for executable friction and dynamics simulations."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, DataVisualizationService, ModelValidationService
]

instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol for real-time sensor data streaming from physical twin to digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, DataVisualizationService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Framework for semantic interoperability and data traceability in federated digital shadow deployments."
    DTDFVocab:enables TraceabilityService, ModelValidationService
]

instance UMLClassDiagramTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "UML class diagrams defining concrete digital shadow model components (FrictionShadow, DataTrace, Processing)."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, DataVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time detection of static, kinetic, and viscous friction in the gearbox via model-based comparison (simulated vs. measured data)."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCharacterizationInsight, PredictiveMaintenanceAction
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integrated dashboard displaying time-series data (torque, angular velocity, current) and friction model deviations."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalDecisionInsight
]

instance TraceabilityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Semantic linking of data traces to their sources (sensors, simulations, models) and processing components."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelValidationInsight
]

instance ModelValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous comparison of white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models to improve accuracy over time."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelImprovementInsight
]

instance RootCauseAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Identification of physical relationships (wear, temperature effects) affecting friction via multi-physics-aware modeling."
    DTDFVocab:provides ResearchInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction components in the gearbox with quantification of friction-induced inaccuracies (up to 30% positional error reduction potential)."
]

instance ModelImprovementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Parameter tuning for white-box models (e.g., adjusting Stribeck curve parameters) and hybrid model selection combining analytical and data-driven approaches."
]

instance OperationalDecisionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance triggers (e.g., detecting abnormal wear patterns) and control strategy adjustments (e.g., compensating for friction in CNC paths)."
]

instance ResearchInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation of multi-physics friction models under real-world conditions and data-driven model refinement for long-term accuracy improvements."
]

instance ModelValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results comparing white-box and black-box friction models to ensure accuracy and reliability of friction analysis."
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of maintenance alerts based on friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlStrategyAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggested adjustments to CNC control paths to compensate for identified friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Environment and Components
instance MABIMAXFirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical environment of the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot's first vertical axis with working range [-90°, 90°] and max angular speed 75°/s."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearWheel, CycloidalGearbox, SINUMERIK840DController,
                 MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder, MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance FrictionShadowDigitalShadow : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized digital shadow instance for the first axis of MABI MAX robot, implementing friction analysis via simulation models."
    base:contains SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagram, SysMLInternalBlockDiagram, ManufacturerDynamicsModel,
                 CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel, DahlFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel,
                 FrictionlessMotorSimulation, SimulationMotor, SimulationGearWheel,
                 MeasuredCurrentData, MeasuredMotorTorqueVelocityData, MeasuredGearWheelTorqueVelocityData,
                 SimulatedMotorTorqueVelocityData, SimulatedGearWheelTorqueVelocityData, SimulatedOutputTorqueVelocityData,
                 DataTraceMetadata
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **DS does not push data back to the PT** (as it is a **digital shadow**, not a digital twin). However, the **insights generated by the DS (e.g., friction identification, model deviations)** can be **indirectly used to improve PT control** via:
  - **Offline analysis** of friction models to refine **white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models**,
  - **Integration with higher-level DT systems** (e.g., a **digital twin**) that may autonomously adjust control parameters or trigger maintenance actions.
  The **SINUMERIK 840D controller** remains the sole active component interfacing with the PT."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Shadow operates on a **real-time scale**, synchronized with the robot's operational data. Sensor data is streamed via **MQTT** in **time-series format**, enabling **live-data linking** for friction model updates. The system is designed for **continuous, dynamic identification** of friction models during operation, with no explicit mention of latency constraints. The **SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller** typically operates at **control loop frequencies of 1–10 ms**, suggesting the DS must align with this timescale for practical integration."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DS is a **standalone, purpose-specific system** within a broader **cyber-physical system (CPS) constellation** that could include:
  - **Physical Layer**: MABI MAX robot (PT) + SINUMERIK 840D controller + encoders.
  - **Virtual Layer**: DS (digital shadow) + **potential digital twin (DT)** for higher-level control or optimization.
  - **Orchestration**:
    - **One-way data flow** from PT → DS (via MQTT),
    - **No direct feedback loop** to PT (unlike a DT),
    - **Integration with upstream systems** (e.g., manufacturing execution systems) via **AAS** for **horizontal integration**.
  The DS could be **extended into a DT** by adding **active components** (e.g., model predictive control) that push corrections back to the PT."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DS covers the **as-operated** lifecycle phase of the PT, focusing on **real-time monitoring and post-facto analysis** of friction effects. It does not model the **as-designed (ideal)** or **as-manufactured** states but uses:
  - **Engineering models** (SysML) to describe the **structural and behavioral design** of the axis,
  - **Simulation models** (Simulink) to represent **dynamic behavior under operational conditions**,
  - **Data traces** from **ongoing operation** to identify **deviations due to wear, temperature, or load changes**.
  The DS is **purpose-driven** (friction analysis) and does not evolve into a **full digital twin** (which would include **predictive or prescriptive actions**)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The DS employs the following **fidelity and validity mechanisms**:
  1. **Model Validation**:
     - **Comparison of simulated vs. measured data** (e.g., τ̂_M vs. τ_M) to quantify **friction effects**.
     - **Uncertainty quantification** via **residual analysis** (differences between models and measurements).
  2. **Traceability**:
     - **End-to-end data provenance** via **metadata** (source, processing component, timestamp).
     - **Transitive traceability** (e.g., a simulated data trace can be traced back to its input traces and models).
  3. **Verification**:
     - **SysML models** are validated against **manufacturer specifications** (e.g., gear ratios, motor constants).
     - **Simulink simulations** are cross-checked with **analytical friction models** (e.g., LuGre for preload effects).
  4. **Error Handling**:
     - **Sensor noise filtering** (e.g., encoder data smoothing),
     - **Model parameter adaptation** to account for **wear or environmental changes** (e.g., temperature-dependent friction).
  **Limitations**:
  - **No direct validation of internal gearbox states** (e.g., gearwheel torque is not directly measurable; inferred via dynamics models).
  - **Assumption of linear elasticity** in the drivetrain (may not hold for extreme loads)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical connection** between PT and DS is established via:
  1. **Network Protocol**: **MQTT** (lightweight, publish-subscribe) for **real-time sensor data streaming** (e.g., I_M, encoder angles).
  2. **Data Format**: **Time-series arrays** of floating-point values (torque, angular velocity) with **metadata** (source, unit, timestamp).
  3. **Architecture**:
     - **Centralized DS instance** hosted on a **local/edge server** (not specified in the document).
     - **No direct PT-DS feedback loop** (unlike a DT), but **data is stored for offline analysis**.
  4. **Interoperability**:
     - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** enables **semantic integration** with other systems (e.g., manufacturing IT).
     - **SysML/Simulink models** are exchanged via **standardized modeling formats** (e.g., XMI for SysML, SLX for Simulink)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The hosting and deployment details are **not explicitly specified** in the document. However, the following can be inferred:
  - **Dashboard**: Used for visualization of **simulated vs. measured data** (likely a web-based or local application).
  - **Simulation Environment**: **MathWorks Simulink** runs on a **local/edge server** (not cloud) for real-time processing.
  - **Data Storage**: Sensor data is streamed via **MQTT** and processed on-the-fly; no explicit mention of databases like **InfluxDB** or deployment containers like **Docker**.
  - **Tools**: **Cameo Systems Modeler** (for SysML) and **Simulink Runtime** may be used but are not confirmed in the text."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration is limited to:
  - **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)**: Used for **semantic integration** of the DS with other systems (e.g., manufacturing execution systems).
  - **MQTT**: Enables **interoperability** with other MQTT-compatible systems (e.g., sensor networks, PLCs).
  The document does not mention **OPC UA**, **MES integration**, or **federated learning**. The DS is primarily **axis-specific** with no explicit plans for broader system integration."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Shadow adheres to the following standards and methodologies:
  - **SysML** (for structural/behavioral modeling),
  - **UML** (for the *Digital Shadow Reference Model*),
  - **MQTT** (for real-time data streaming),
  - **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** (for semantic interoperability).
  No explicit mention of **FMI**, **IEC 61131-3**, **ISO 10444**, or **INCOSE standards** is provided."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Safety and security considerations include:
  - **Passive Operation**: The DS does not autonomously act on the physical system, reducing direct safety risks.
  - **Safety Cell**: The robot’s **working range is restricted to [-90°, 90°]** by a physical safety cell, mitigating collision risks.
  - **No Remote Execution**: The DS does not execute operations remotely; insights are used **indirectly** (e.g., for control improvements).
  The document does not address **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., TLS/SSL, OAuth2), **compliance standards** (e.g., ISO 26262, IEC 62443), or **access control mechanisms**."
]
